candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue line; exact match to total revenue/sales and preferable to duplicate total row.
Duplicate total revenue line with identical values; useful as fallback if label changes by year.
Not revenue; bottom-line comprehensive income.
Balance sheet working-capital item, not revenue.
Contract/receivable-related asset, not total revenue.
Liability/accrual line, not revenue.
Deferred revenue-related liability, not top-line revenue.

variables.notes: Using both revenue labels as a priority list because they are synonymous and may vary by year; later code should take the first non-missing value.

candidates.why: Best match to direct operating/property-level costs; appears to be the main cost-of-sales style line.
Only a subcomponent of property costs, not the full direct cost bucket.

variables.notes: Selected the broader direct cost line rather than a subcomponent. Excluded Operating & Maintenance / Real Estate Taxes because they were not in the candidate list and the broader Property Costs row appears to aggregate them.

candidates.why: Clear overhead/administrative expense component; strong SG&A match.
Selling/marketing overhead component appropriate for SG&A.

variables.notes: No explicit single SG&A subtotal is shown. Chose overhead components only and excluded balancing/total rows, COGS-like property costs, and D&A.
No dedicated research and development line is present on the income statement.

candidates.why: Finance expense total, but overlaps with more granular interest expense; less preferred when component exists.
Most direct and preferred recurring interest cost line.
May include non-interest or mixed finance items; less precise than interest expense.
Not financing cost.
Equity/NCI line, not interest expense.
NCI line, not interest expense.
Liability balance, not period interest expense.
Balance sheet accrual, not income statement expense.

variables.notes: Preferred the granular recurring interest expense row and avoided double counting with broader Financial Expense.

candidates.why: Income statement profit attribution line, not equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to Parent Company shareholders
candidates.why: Best match for book equity excluding non-controlling interests; exactly follows the BE rule.
This is NCI/MIB, not parent book equity.
Usable only if no parent-only equity line exists; here a better parent-only line exists.
Shareholder count, not equity.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to Parent Company shareholders

variables.notes: Selected parent-only equity to avoid double counting non-controlling interests in BE + MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Direct balance sheet non-controlling interest line; best match.
Also a direct balance sheet minority interest label; suitable fallback.
Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.

variables.notes: Using both balance-sheet NCI labels as a priority list because they are synonymous and may vary by year; values shown are zero.
